name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands shoulder-width apart and feet together. Lower your body until your chest almost touches the floor, keeping your body in a straight line. Push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body.
Engage your core to maintain a straight line from head to heels.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or piking your hips up.
Not maintaining a straight line from your head to your heels.

name: Squat
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up. Go down until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to standing.
tips: Keep your knees aligned over your toes.
Engage your core throughout the movement.
mistakes: Letting your knees cave inward.
Not going low enough to engage your muscles.

name: Plank
content: Lie face down and lift your body off the ground with your forearms and toes. Keep your body straight and hold the position, engaging your core and glutes.
tips: Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Breathe deeply and maintain tension in your core.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Holding your breath instead of breathing steadily.

name: Lunges
content: Stand upright and step forward with one leg, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push back to the starting position and repeat on the other leg.
tips: Keep your front knee over your ankle.
Engage your core to maintain balance.
mistakes: Letting your front knee extend beyond your toes.
Not keeping your back straight.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
content: Stand or sit with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Press the weights overhead until your arms are fully extended, then lower back to the start.
tips: Keep your back straight and avoid arching.
Engage your core for stability.
mistakes: Using momentum to lift the weights.
Letting your elbows flare out too much.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, with the barbell over your midfoot. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the bar, then stand up by extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
tips: Keep your back flat and chest up.
Drive through your heels as you lift.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Lifting with your back instead of your legs.

name: Bent-Over Row
content: Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight. Hold the dumbbells or barbell with an overhand grip. Pull the weights towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower them back down.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
Engage your core to maintain stability.
mistakes: Using momentum to lift the weights.
Rounding your back instead of keeping it straight.

name: Bicycle Crunch
content: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head. Bring your knees to a 90-degree angle. Lift your shoulders off the ground and rotate your torso, bringing your right elbow to your left knee while straightening your right leg. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion.
tips: Keep your lower back pressed against the floor.
Move in a controlled manner to maximize engagement.
mistakes: Pulling on your neck with your hands.
Rushing through the movements.

name: Jumping Jacks
content: Stand upright with your arms at your sides. Jump up, spreading your legs shoulder-width apart while raising your arms overhead. Jump back to the starting position.
tips: Land softly on your toes to reduce impact.
Engage your core to maintain stability.
mistakes: Not fully extending your arms and legs.
Landing heavily, which can strain your joints.

name: Glute Bridge
content: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Lift your hips towards the ceiling by squeezing your glutes, then lower back down without touching the floor.
tips: Keep your shoulders and head on the ground.
Engage your core throughout the lift.
mistakes: Arching your back instead of lifting with your glutes.
Not squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core throughout the movement.
If needed, perform on your knees for a modified version.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise excessively.
Looking down instead of slightly ahead.
Flaring your elbows out too much.

name: Squat
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if sitting in a chair. Keep your chest up and back straight. Return to standing.
tips: Keep your weight on your heels.
Push your knees out, not allowing them to cave inward.
Use a mirror to check your form.
mistakes: Allowing your knees to go past your toes.
Lifting your heels off the ground.
Not going low enough for a proper squat.

name: Plank
content: Lie face down, then lift your body off the ground with your forearms and toes. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels and hold the position.
tips: Engage your core and squeeze your glutes.
Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Breathe steadily while maintaining your position.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Holding your breath.
Looking straight down instead of slightly ahead.

name: Lunges
content: Stand tall, take a step forward with one leg, and lower your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push back to the starting position and switch legs.
tips: Keep your front knee above your ankle.
Engage your core and maintain an upright torso.
Alternate legs for balanced training.
mistakes: Leaning forward excessively.
Allowing your knee to extend past your toes.
Not stepping far enough forward.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
content: Sit or stand with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended, then lower back to the starting position.
tips: Keep your back straight and avoid arching.
Don’t lock your elbows at the top.
Use a controlled motion to prevent injury.
mistakes: Using momentum to lift the weights.
Dropping the weights too quickly.
Letting your elbows flare out excessively.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, with the barbell over the middle of your feet. Bend at your hips and knees to grab the bar. Keep your back straight and lift the bar by straightening your legs and hips.
tips: Keep the bar close to your body throughout the lift.
Engage your core and keep your chest up.
Start with lighter weights to master form.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Letting the bar drift away from your body.
Not engaging your core.

name: Bicycle Crunches
content: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head. Bring your knees to a 90-degree angle and lift your shoulders off the ground. Alternate bringing your elbow to the opposite knee while straightening the other leg.
tips: Keep a steady rhythm and engage your core.
Avoid pulling on your neck.
Exhale as you crunch to maximize contraction.
mistakes: Rushing through the movement.
Losing contact with your back on the ground.
Pulling your head with your hands.

name: Bent-over Row
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding weights. Bend slightly at the knees and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight. Pull the weights towards your hips, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Control the weights on the way down.
mistakes: Rounding your back.
Lifting weights that are too heavy.
Not fully extending your arms at the bottom.

name: Tricep Dips
content: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands next to your hips. Move your hips forward off the bench and lower your body by bending your elbows to about a 90-degree angle, then push back up.
tips: Keep your elbows pointed back, not out to the sides.
Maintain a straight line from your head to your knees.
Engage your core for stability.
mistakes: Allowing your shoulders to hunch.
Not going low enough for a proper dip.
Using your legs to push instead of your arms.

name: Russian Twist
content: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat. Lean back slightly and hold a weight with both hands. Twist your torso to one side, then back to the center, and then to the other side.
tips: Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Move your arms, not just your torso.
Control the motion to avoid injury.
mistakes: Rounding your back.
Twisting too quickly or violently.
Not engaging your core.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a high plank position with your hands placed shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, keeping your elbows close to your body. Push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core throughout the movement.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Flaring your elbows out too much.

name: Squat
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if sitting in a chair. Keep your chest up and back straight. Return to standing.
tips: Keep your knees aligned with your toes.
Go as low as you can while maintaining good form.
mistakes: Allowing your knees to cave inward.
Lifting your heels off the ground.

name: Plank
content: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows directly under your shoulders. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Hold this position for as long as you can.
tips: Keep your core tight and avoid sagging your hips.
Breathe steadily while holding the position.
mistakes: Allowing your lower back to sag.
Holding your breath.

name: Lunges
content: Stand upright, then take a step forward with one leg. Lower your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push back to the starting position and alternate legs.
tips: Keep your front knee over your ankle.
Engage your core for stability.
mistakes: Letting your front knee go past your toes.
Failing to keep your back straight.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, barbell over your midfoot. Bend at the hips and knees to grab the barbell with both hands. Keep your back straight as you lift the bar by extending your hips and knees together.
tips: Keep the bar close to your body during the lift.
Drive through your heels.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Lifting with your back instead of your legs.

name: Bench Press
content: Lie flat on a bench with your feet on the ground. Grab the barbell with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower the bar to your chest, then press it back up until your arms are fully extended.
tips: Keep your elbows at about a 45-degree angle.
Engage your feet to maintain stability.
mistakes: Arching your back excessively.
Bouncing the bar off your chest.

name: Pull-Up
content: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you at shoulder-width. Pull your body up until your chin is above the bar, then lower yourself back down.
tips: Engage your shoulders before pulling.
Keep your body straight throughout the movement.
mistakes: Swinging your legs for momentum.
Not fully extending your arms at the bottom.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
content: Stand or sit with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended. Lower back to shoulder height.
tips: Keep your core engaged and back straight.
Avoid arching your back.
mistakes: Letting your elbows flare out too much.
Using momentum to lift the weights.

name: Bicycle Crunches
content: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and legs lifted at a 90-degree angle. Bring your right elbow toward your left knee while straightening your right leg. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion.
tips: Keep your lower back pressed into the ground.
Move slowly to maximize tension in your abs.
mistakes: Pulling on your neck with your hands.
Rushing through the movement.

name: Glute Bridge
content: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Lift your hips toward the ceiling until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Lower back down.
tips: Engage your glutes at the top of the movement.
Keep your shoulders relaxed.
mistakes: Letting your knees fall outward.
Raising your shoulders off the ground.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core throughout the movement.
Breathe in as you lower down and exhale as you push up.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise towards the ceiling.
Flaring your elbows out too wide.
Not going low enough; aim for your chest to touch the ground.

name: Squat
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up. Go as low as you can while maintaining good form, then return to standing.
tips: Keep your weight on your heels as you squat.
Ensure your knees do not go past your toes.
Engage your core to support your back.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the squat.
Allowing your knees to cave inward.
Not going low enough; aim for thighs parallel to the ground.

name: Plank
content: Lie face down, then lift your body off the ground with your forearms and toes. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels and hold this position.
tips: Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes.
Breathe steadily and avoid holding your breath.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Looking up or down instead of straight ahead.
Holding your breath instead of breathing steadily.

name: Dumbbell Row
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand. Bend forward slightly at the hips, keeping your back straight. Pull the dumbbell towards your hip, squeezing your shoulder blade at the top, then lower it back down.
tips: Keep your back flat and avoid rounding your shoulders.
Pull with your back rather than your arms.
Keep your elbow close to your body as you row.
mistakes: Using momentum instead of muscle to lift the weight.
Twisting your torso as you pull the weight.
Letting your shoulder drop instead of keeping it engaged.

name: Lunges
content: Stand upright, then step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push back to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
tips: Keep your front knee over your ankle.
Engage your core to maintain balance.
Push through the heel of your front foot to return.
mistakes: Letting your front knee go past your toes.
Leaning forward excessively.
Not maintaining a straight back.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, barbell over your mid-foot. Bend at your hips and knees to grab the bar. Keeping your back flat, lift the bar by pushing through your heels and straightening your hips and knees.
tips: Keep the bar close to your body throughout the lift.
Engage your core to protect your back.
Avoid rounding your back; keep it straight.
mistakes: Lifting with your back instead of your legs.
Letting the bar drift away from your body.
Not engaging your core for stability.

name: Bicycle Crunches
content: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head. Lift your legs and alternate bringing your elbow to the opposite knee while extending the other leg out. Keep your movements controlled.
tips: Keep your lower back pressed into the ground.
Engage your core throughout the exercise.
Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands.
mistakes: Using momentum instead of controlled movements.
Letting your lower back arch off the ground.
Pulling on your neck instead of using your abs.

name: Burpees
content: Start standing, drop into a squat position, kick your feet back into a plank, perform a push-up, then jump back to squat and explode into a jump.
tips: Maintain a steady rhythm throughout the movement.
Land softly on your feet from the jump.
Use your arms to help propel you upward.
mistakes: Skipping the push-up to rush through the exercise.
Not landing softly, which can lead to injury.
Allowing your form to deteriorate when fatigued.

name: Jumping Jacks
content: Stand upright, then jump while spreading your legs and raising your arms overhead. Jump back to the starting position and repeat.
tips: Keep your core engaged to maintain balance.
Land softly to reduce impact on your joints.
Maintain a steady pace to keep your heart rate up.
mistakes: Rounding your back while jumping.
Not using your arms effectively.
Jumping too high and landing hard.

name: Tricep Dips
content: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands next to your thighs. Slide your butt off the edge and lower your body by bending your elbows, then push back up.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
Lower yourself until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core to support your body.
mistakes: Letting your elbows flare out.
Not lowering down enough; aim for 90 degrees.
Rounding your shoulders instead of keeping them back.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, keeping your elbows close to your body. Push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core to maintain stability.
Breathe out as you push up and in as you lower down.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Flaring your elbows out too wide.
Not going down low enough.

name: Squat
content: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if sitting in a chair. Keep your chest up and knees aligned with your toes. Return to standing.
tips: Keep your weight on your heels.
Go as low as your flexibility allows while maintaining form.
Use your arms for balance.
mistakes: Allowing your knees to cave inward.
Lifting your heels off the ground.
Not keeping your chest up.

name: Plank
content: Lie face down and lift your body off the ground with your forearms and toes. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Hold the position for as long as possible.
tips: Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and glutes.
Breathe steadily and avoid holding your breath.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise.
Looking forward instead of down.
Tensing your neck excessively.

name: Dumbbell Row
content: Bend at the waist with a dumbbell in each hand. Keep your back straight and pull the weights towards your lower rib cage. Lower them back down with control.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Maintain a neutral spine throughout.
mistakes: Using momentum to lift the weights.
Hunching your shoulders.
Not controlling the weight on the way down.

name: Lunges
content: Stand tall and take a step forward with one leg. Lower your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push through the front heel to return to starting position and repeat on the other leg.
tips: Keep your front knee aligned over your ankle.
Engage your core for balance.
Alternate legs for a balanced workout.
mistakes: Letting your front knee move past your toes.
Rushing through the movement.
Not maintaining an upright posture.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with feet hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet. Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar. Keep your back flat and drive through your heels to lift the bar while keeping it close to your body.
tips: Keep your chest up and shoulders back.
Engage your core before lifting.
Lower the bar with control.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Lifting with your back instead of your legs.
Not keeping the bar close to your body.

name: Bicycle Crunch
content: Lie on your back and bring your knees up to a tabletop position. Place your hands behind your head and lift your shoulders off the ground. Alternate bringing your opposite elbow to your knee while extending the other leg.
tips: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
Move in a controlled manner to maximize muscle engagement.
Breathe out as you crunch and in as you extend.
mistakes: Pulling on your neck with your hands.
Rushing through the movement.
Letting your lower back arch.

name: Bench Press
content: Lie on a bench with your feet flat on the ground. Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width and lower it to your chest. Push the bar back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your feet planted and back flat on the bench.
Lower the bar to your mid-chest area.
Exhale as you push the weight up.
mistakes: Bouncing the bar off your chest.
Lifting your feet off the ground.
Flaring your elbows out excessively.

name: Tricep Dip
content: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands next to your hips. Slide your butt off the bench and lower your body by bending your elbows. Push back up until your arms are straight.
tips: Keep your elbows pointing backward.
Lower your body until your arms are at a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core for stability.
mistakes: Allowing your elbows to flare out.
Using your legs to push instead of your arms.
Not going low enough to engage the triceps.

name: Mountain Climbers
content: Start in a plank position. Quickly drive one knee towards your chest, then switch legs in a running motion while keeping your core engaged and hips down.
tips: Maintain a steady rhythm for endurance.
Keep your back straight and avoid sagging hips.
Breathe steadily throughout the exercise.
mistakes: Letting your hips rise too high.
Not keeping your core engaged.
Rushing the movement which can lead to losing form.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body.
Engage your core to maintain body alignment.
Breathe in as you lower down and exhale as you push up.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Flaring elbows out too much during the descent.
Not going low enough to engage the chest muscles.

name: Squat
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower your body as if sitting in a chair. Keep your chest up and knees aligned with your toes. Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to standing.
tips: Keep your weight in your heels.
Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Look straight ahead to help with balance.
mistakes: Allowing knees to cave inward.
Rounding your back during the squat.
Lifting your heels off the ground.

name: Plank
content: Start on your hands and knees, then extend your legs back to come into a plank position. Your hands should be directly under your shoulders, and your body should form a straight line from head to heels. Hold this position while engaging your core.
tips: Keep your body straight and avoid sagging your hips.
Breathe steadily and maintain tension in your core.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, gradually increasing the duration.
mistakes: Letting your hips drop or rise too high.
Holding your breath instead of breathing steadily.
Looking too far up or down, which can strain the neck.

name: Lunges
content: Stand with your feet together. Step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. The back knee should hover just above the ground. Push back to the starting position and switch legs.
tips: Keep your front knee aligned with your ankle.
Engage your core to maintain balance.
Alternate legs for equal training.
mistakes: Letting the front knee extend past the toes.
Leaning forward instead of keeping your torso upright.
Not stepping far enough forward.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
content: Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Hold the dumbbells above your chest with arms extended. Lower the weights to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at about a 45-degree angle. Push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your feet flat on the ground for stability.
Control the movement during both the lowering and lifting phases.
Avoid locking your elbows at the top.
mistakes: Using too much weight, which can compromise form.
Raising your feet off the ground, leading to instability.
Allowing dumbbells to drift too far apart.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, with the barbell close to your shins. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the bar. Keep your back flat, chest up, and shoulders back. Push through your heels to lift the bar while keeping it close to your body. Stand tall at the top, then lower back down.
tips: Maintain a neutral spine throughout the lift.
Engage your core to protect your lower back.
Use a mixed grip if the bar slips from your hands.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Lifting with your back instead of your legs.
Letting the bar drift away from your body.

name: Bicycle Crunch
content: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and legs lifted at a 90-degree angle. Bring one elbow to the opposite knee while extending the other leg. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion while engaging your core.
tips: Control your movements to maximize tension on your abs.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
Breathe out as you twist and engage your abs.
mistakes: Pulling on your neck with your hands.
Not fully extending your legs, reducing effectiveness.
Rushing through the movement instead of maintaining control.

name: Jump Squat
content: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a squat and explode upwards, jumping as high as possible. Land softly back into the squat position and immediately go into the next jump.
tips: Use your arms to help generate momentum.
Land softly to reduce impact on your joints.
Keep your core engaged for stability.
mistakes: Landing hard on your heels, which can cause injury.
Squatting too low before jumping.
Not maintaining balance upon landing.

name: Overhead Press
content: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Press the weights overhead until your arms are fully extended. Lower the weights back to shoulder height with control.
tips: Keep your core tight to support your back.
Avoid arching your back during the lift.
Use a staggered stance for better balance if needed.
mistakes: Raising your back excessively, which can lead to injury.
Not fully extending your arms at the top.
Letting weights drift forward or backward instead of staying aligned.

name: Mountain Climbers
content: Start in a plank position. Quickly drive one knee towards your chest, then switch legs in a running motion. Keep your core engaged and back flat throughout the movement.
tips: Maintain a steady pace to keep your heart rate up.
Focus on driving your knees high for better engagement.
Breathe steadily, exhaling with each knee drive.
mistakes: Letting your hips sag or rise excessively.
Running too fast and losing form.
Not keeping your core engaged, which can lead to back strain.

name: Push-Up
content: Start in a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core to maintain stability.
Avoid letting your hips sag or rise.
mistakes: Allowing your hips to drop, which can strain your lower back.
Flaring your elbows out too wide, which can put unnecessary stress on your shoulders.
Not going low enough, limiting the effectiveness of the exercise.

name: Squat
content: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and back straight. Go as low as you can while maintaining good form, then return to standing.
tips: Keep your weight on your heels to avoid knee strain.
Engage your core throughout the movement.
Breathe in as you lower down and exhale as you push up.
mistakes: Letting your knees travel too far forward over your toes.
Rounding your back, which can lead to injury.
Not lowering down enough, which reduces the effectiveness of the squat.

name: Plank
content: Lie face down, then lift your body off the ground with your forearms and toes, forming a straight line from head to heels. Hold this position while engaging your core muscles.
tips: Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Avoid letting your hips sag or rise too high.
Breathe steadily while maintaining the position.
mistakes: Holding your breath, which can lead to fatigue.
Allowing your back to sag, which can strain your spine.
Not engaging your core, reducing the effectiveness of the exercise.

name: Lunges
content: Stand tall, then step forward with one leg, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about a 90-degree angle. Push back to the starting position and switch legs.
tips: Keep your front knee directly above your ankle.
Engage your core for balance.
Push through the heel of your front foot to return to standing.
mistakes: Letting your front knee extend past your toes.
Leaning forward excessively, which can strain your back.
Not maintaining balance, which can lead to falls.

name: Bent-Over Row
content: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight. Pull the weights towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower back down.
tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Start with lighter weights to ensure proper form.
mistakes: Rounding your back, which can cause injury.
Using momentum to lift the weights instead of controlled movement.
Letting your elbows flare out excessively.

name: Deadlift
content: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, with the barbell over your mid-foot. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the bar, keeping your back straight. Drive through your heels to lift the bar while keeping it close to your body, then lower back down.
tips: Engage your core before lifting.
Keep the bar path straight and close to your body.
Exhale as you lift and inhale as you lower.
mistakes: Rounding your back during the lift.
Not keeping the bar close to your body, leading to inefficient lifting.
Lifting with your back instead of your legs.

name: Bicycle Crunch

To gain muscle, focus on progressive overload by gradually increasing the weights you lift and ensuring adequate protein intake to support muscle repair.

Incorporating whole foods like fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains can boost your nutrient intake and support weight management.

Compound exercises, such as squats, deadlifts, and bench presses, are effective for building muscle as they work multiple muscle groups simultaneously.
Establishing a regular sleep schedule and creating a relaxing bedtime routine can enhance sleep quality and support recovery from workouts.
